pi_items.source: Code analysis: seed_library.py creates collection via ml_with_collection mode; setup_task.sh verifies collection exists in database
Code analysis: export_result.sh checks for file at this primary path
Code analysis: README.md explicitly documents 8 papers in 'ML References' collection; seed_library.py ML_PAPERS list contains exactly 8 papers

Web search verification: All 8 authors confirmed via peer-reviewed paper citations. (1) Vaswani et al. 'Attention Is All You Need' NIPS 2017 (arxiv 1706.03762); (2) Devlin et al. 'BERT' NAACL 2019 (arxiv 1810.04805); (3) Brown et al. 'Language Models are Few-Shot Learners' NeurIPS 2020 (arxiv 2005.14165); (4) Krizhevsky et al. 'ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks' NIPS 2012; (5) He et al. 'Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition' CVPR 2016 (arxiv 1512.03385); (6) Goodfellow et al. 'Generative Adversarial Nets' NIPS 2014 (arxiv 1406.2661); (7) LeCun, Bengio, Hinton 'Deep Learning' Nature 2015 (doi 10.1038/nature14539); (8) Silver et al. 'Mastering the Game of Go' Nature 2016

privileged_info_summary: The task exports a synthetic ML References collection containing 8 foundational deep learning papers to BibTeX format at /home/ga/Desktop/ml_bibliography.bib. All 8 expected authors (Vaswani, Devlin, Brown, Krizhevsky, He, Goodfellow, LeCun, Silver) are verified as primary authors of real, peer-reviewed papers from 2012-2020. However, the metadata claim of expected_min_entries=5 is contradicted by evidence: the collection actually contains exactly 8 papers as documented in README.md and configured in seed_library.py.
